hook: AI gave me a 47-step plan when I asked for help with one decision. 🙃
caption:
AI gave me a 47-step plan when I asked for help with one decision. 🙃

You asked: "Should I take this freelance project?"

Generic AI gave you: A framework. With phases. And sub-bullets. And a note to "consult relevant stakeholders."

You have no stakeholders. You're in your kitchen at 11pm trying to make ONE call.

Here's the thing — AI isn't broken. It just doesn't know that you're a gut-trust-first, give-me-the-punchline, don't-explain-what-I-already-know kind of thinker.

So it defaults to thorough. Comprehensive. Safe.

Which feels like asking your friend for a restaurant rec and getting a Yelp spreadsheet.

The fix isn't a better prompt every single time. It's teaching the AI how your brain actually works — once — so every response fits the way you think, not the way a committee decided "helpful" should look.

That's what a BootFile does. It's your thinking style, translated into a profile the AI loads before it ever types a word back to you.

Short-answer people get short answers.
Big-picture thinkers get the forest, not every tree.
Skeptics get the tradeoffs, not the sales pitch.

One decision. One answer. No phases.
